prompt: The gift-card counter's magstripe decoder has been quietly wrong for a while and now it's loudly wrong too. Retail fleet runs every kiosk service under strict interpreter flags these days, and under them the decoder's gate — `python3 -bb -W error test_magstripe.py` — won't even start; the interpreter objects to the module before a single swipe is decoded. And the misbehavior we'd been chalking up to flaky pen hardware lines up suspiciously well: cards that are definitely status-A get treated as blocked, every single card lands in the 'unknown' tier no matter its prefix, decoded card ids sometimes come out with a stray ';' glued on the front, and the end-of-batch marker the pen prints is never recognized so batches never close out. The pen hands us raw bytes over serial — that part is fine and shouldn't change. Expected decode results are all pinned in the gate test. Please get the gate passing under those exact flags without loosening them; test_magstripe.py is frozen.
notes: 5W1 item 6: BytesWarning + comparison warnings, CPython 3.13.11. VERIFY-FLAG DEVIATION from catalog, probed on this box: `-b -W error::BytesWarning` does NOT make the comparison fatal (the warning prints and execution continues, exit 0), while `-bb` raises BytesWarning directly — so the gate uses `python3 -bb -W error` (same '-b-style strictness' intent, plus -W error keeps every other warning class fatal). Defects: (1) str/bytes comparisons x3 — strip_sentinels compares raw[:1]/raw[-1:] (bytes) against str sentinels (both always False, so ';' leaks into card ids), and batch_done compares a stripped serial line (bytes) against '*' (batches never close); silent wrong-answers without -b, BytesWarning errors under -bb. (2) `is` vs `==` on literals x2 in tier() — SyntaxWarning at module compile time, fatal at import under -W error (the 'won't even start' symptom); behaviorally the sliced prefix is a fresh str object so `is` is False and every card tiers as 'unknown'. is_active compares a decoded str status against b'A' (active cards read blocked). Fixes: compare bytes with bytes (b';', b'?', b'*'), str with str ('A'), and == for the prefix literals; tests pin all decode outputs exactly, including tracks with and without sentinels. Test file itself is -bb-clean (no mixed-type comparisons). Domain (magstripe pen reader at a retail counter) doesn't overlap any existing seed.
